title: Zillow Group Stock Forecast & Analyst Predictions
text: Zillow Housing Market Predictions and Quarterly Outlook Analysis. Current National Outlook: Zillow's updated 12-month forecast projects that U.S. home prices will shift 0.0% between March 2026 and March 2027. This represents a downward revision from previous forecasts, indicating a soft national housing market. Home Values and Sales: Home values are expected to grow by 1.9%, with the typical U.S. home value ending 2026 at $365,795. Zillow projects 4.26 million existing home sales next year — a 4.3% increase over 2025 — as improving affordability brings more demand back to the market. Affordability and Mortgage Rates: A mortgage payment on a typical home is forecast to be affordable in 20 major metropolitan areas by the end of 2026, the most since 2022. Mortgage rates are unlikely to fall below 6% in 2026. Rental Market: Zillow forecasts multifamily rents to rise just 0.3% in 2026, giving incomes a chance to catch up. Single-family rents are projected to climb 2.3% as many buyers delay home purchases. Market Conditions: Zillow is calling for a soft national housing market in 2026, one where national housing affordability may improve slightly as U.S. income growth outpaces U.S. home price growth. However, Zillow advises thinking about 2026 not as a single forecast but as a range of possible scenarios, each shaped by how key economic factors evolve over time.
